T0056.title: Car Loan Cash-Out
T0056.definition: Car financing fraud refers to the act of using falsified documents to fraudulently obtain an auto loan and then cashing out the vehicle.
T0056.description: Fraud rings recruit credit mules with clean records and fabricate their employment and income proofs. They use these fake profiles to secure a loan from a financial institution under the pretense of buying a car. Once the vehicle is acquired, it is not used but is immediately pawned or sold for cash. The loan is then abandoned, resulting in a bad debt for the auto loan.

references.title: 2024 H1 Credit Fraud Risk Landscape Report
Black Market Big Data: Exposing Malicious Loan Brokers
